message:
raptorq: surface regression-low-confidence fallback reason and harden choose_action

Promote the deterministic fallback reason strings to named constants in
src/raptorq/decision_contract.rs and wire a new
`regression_state_with_low_confidence` reason that was previously colliding
with the generic `conservative_fallback_reason_unclassified` bucket. This
lets telemetry distinguish the regression+low-confidence branch from the
truly unclassified fallback case, which matters for Track-G governance
since the two paths have different upstream remediation expectations.

Refactor `choose_action` to delegate to the new
`choose_action_from_expected_loss_terms` helper so the G7 deterministic
tie-breaker logic can be reused directly against an
`expected_loss_terms` array. The governance telemetry path needs to
validate the chosen action matches the published loss terms without
re-running the full loss matrix, and a new surface test
(`surfaced_expected_loss_ties_drive_the_reported_action`) asserts the
helper and the telemetry agree on a regression fixture that surfaces a
CONTINUE/CANARY_HOLD tie.

Add fail-closed coverage for malformed inputs to the decision contract:

- `choose_action_fails_closed_for_malformed_posterior_length` pins the
  behavior where a 3-state posterior passed into a 4-state contract
  falls back to action::FALLBACK rather than panicking.
- `posterior_update_ignores_malformed_length` pins the same guarantee
  for the Bayesian update path.
- `posterior_update_ignores_out_of_range_observation` covers the
  usize::MAX observation index against a well-formed posterior so
  the index guard cannot regress to an implicit modular wrap.

Extend tests/raptorq_perf_invariants.rs
(`g7_expected_loss_contract_schema_and_coverage`) to enforce that the
checked-in G7 expected-loss contract artifact publishes the full set
of fallback trigger reasons including the new
regression_state_with_low_confidence entry, via a BTreeSet membership
check so the schema and the Rust constants cannot silently drift.

Bump artifacts/raptorq_expected_loss_decision_contract_v1.json to
include regression_state_with_low_confidence in the
deterministic_fallback_trigger.if_any list and refresh generated_at_utc
to 2026-04-11T18:06:45Z so the artifact aligns with the current code
and test expectations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -3917,6 +3917,30 @@ fn g7_expected_loss_contract_schema_and_coverage() {
         Some("fallback"),
         "G7 fallback trigger must force fallback action"
     );
+    let fallback_trigger_reasons =
+        artifact["contract"]["decision_rule"]["deterministic_fallback_trigger"]["if_any"]
+            .as_array()
+            .expect("G7 fallback trigger if_any must be an array")
+            .iter()
+            .map(|value| {
+                value
+                    .as_str()
+                    .expect("G7 fallback trigger reasons must be strings")
+                    .to_string()
+            })
+            .collect::<BTreeSet<_>>();
+    for reason in [
+        "decode_mismatch_detected",
+        "proof_replay_mismatch",
+        "unknown_state_with_low_confidence",
+        "regression_state_with_low_confidence",
+        "conservative_fallback_reason_unclassified",
+    ] {
+        assert!(
+            fallback_trigger_reasons.contains(reason),
+            "G7 fallback trigger contract must include {reason}"
+        );
+    }
 
     let expected_levers = BTreeSet::from([
         "C5".to_string(),
